assumptions: The developer has Node.js 20.19 or later and a local command line.
The developer does not yet have an Algolia account, application, or index.
The developer can run a local Vite development server and open localhost:5173.

developer_goal: Create an Algolia account, index the sample product dataset, and reach a working React InstantSearch experience.

first_success.outcome: A local React InstantSearch app queries the quickstart-products index and shows live product search results.
first_success.observable_signal: At http://localhost:5173 the product grid updates while typing, and the guide states you now have a working end-to-end search.

route_selection.surface: Algolia dashboard plus Build your first search experience quickstart
route_selection.rule: Start at Algolia account creation, then follow the Get started page's Start building React InstantSearch quickstart rather than AI-agent, CLI, or Shopify peers.

candidates.name: Work Email signup then React InstantSearch quickstart
candidates.reason: Public signup exposes Work Email, password minimum, and terms gates, but post-submit verification and whether an application is auto-created remain undocumented.

candidates.name: Google, GitHub, or Vercel signup then React InstantSearch quickstart
candidates.reason: These are peer identity choices on the signup page with undocumented return states into the dashboard.

candidates.name: Build with AI agents
candidates.reason: Get started separates this from the Start building InstantSearch quickstart.

candidates.name: Algolia CLI or Shopify quickstart
candidates.reason: Different surfaces from the general first search-experience route.

required_path.action: Create an Algolia account from the free signup page using Work Email or a supported social identity.

required_fields.label: Signup method
required_fields.notes: Google, GitHub, Vercel, and Work Email are peer options.

required_fields.label: Password
required_fields.notes: Public UI indicates at least eight characters on Work Email.

required_fields.label: Terms of Service, Build Plan Terms, and Privacy Policy acceptance

required_path.observable_result: The developer can open the Algolia dashboard for a new account.

required_path.action: Open or create the Algolia application whose API keys will be used, then copy Application ID, Write API key, and Search API key.

required_fields.label: Application selection or creation fields
required_fields.notes: Quickstart assumes an application; help docs describe manual creation without saying when new accounts need it.

required_path.observable_result: The three credential values are available to paste into .env.local.

required_path.action: Create the Vite React TypeScript app, install dependencies, add credentials, and run the indexing script.
required_path.observable_result: The terminal reports Successfully indexed products and the quickstart-products index exists.

required_path.action: Replace App.tsx and App.css with the quickstart InstantSearch UI, start the dev server, and search in the browser.
required_path.observable_result: localhost:5173 shows a working product search grid with filters and highlighting.

prerequisites.description: Node.js 20.19 or later is required before the Vite and indexing commands.

external_gates.description: Account creation requires accepting Algolia's published terms and privacy notice on Work Email signup.

unavoidable_waits.description: Indexing uses asynchronous Algolia tasks; the script waits with waitForTasks before succeeding.

platform_outcomes.description: Algolia stores the uploaded sample products in quickstart-products and applies the product_type facet setting.

excluded.item: Relevance tuning and insights instrumentation after first success
excluded.reason: Presented after the working end-to-end search declaration.

excluded.item: Downloading the complete demo from GitHub instead of building it
excluded.reason: Optional exploration parallel to the step-by-step path.

sources.title: Algolia get started for free signup
evidence_supported: Work Email and social signup choices
Password and terms gates visible publicly

sources.title: Get started with Algolia
evidence_supported: Start building points to the InstantSearch quickstart

sources.title: How do I create an application?
evidence_supported: Does not state when a brand-new account already has an app

uncertainties.question: Does a newly created Algolia account automatically receive its first application, or must the developer create one before API Keys are available?
uncertainties.impact: Application creation may add name, plan, billing, and region fields before credentials can be copied.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A fresh-account dashboard walkthrough from signup submit through the first API Keys page.

uncertainties.question: What verification or onboarding screens follow Work Email versus Google, GitHub, and Vercel signup?
uncertainties.impact: Additional account actions or waits may precede dashboard access and change the required_path length.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Dated captures of each signup method through first authenticated dashboard view.
